sections.text: Arcane Intention is an Arcane Enhancement that increases maximum health for every active channeled ability. Effect Rank Max Health Bonus 0 +40 1 +80 2 +120 3 +160 4 +200 5 +250 Acquisition May appear as a Steel Path Circuit Tier 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, or +11 reward on a weekly rotational basis. May be sold by Acrithis for 10 Pathos Clamp on a daily rotational basis, requiring Drifter Intrinsics Opportunity Rank 9 for the arcanes to appear in her shop. One of possible rewards from completing the optional Undercroft Portals in Duviri while on the Steel Path. Notes The bonus stacks for every channeled ability simultaneously active, up to a maximum of 4 stacks (+1000 Health at max rank). Besides increasing maximum health, activating this Arcane also heals the Warframe by the same amount of hitpoints. After the ability that sustained this Arcane's effect ends, there is a 1 second delay during which said ability cannot be reactivated. Eligible Abilities Only the following abilities can trigger this effect: Warframe Ability Notes Atlas Tectonics Does not qualify if Tectonic Fracture is used. Rumblers Banshee Sound Quake Does not qualify if Resonating Quake is used. Baruuk Elude Serene Storm Chroma Spectral Scream Effigy Cyte-09 Neutralize Dante Noctua Ember Immolation Equinox Pacify & Provoke Mend & Maim Excalibur Exalted Blade Gara Mass Vitrify Only qualifies during the glass wall expansion. Gauss Mach Rush Kinetic Plating Redline Grendel Pulverize Gyre Arcsphere Only qualifies while the gyratory sphere is in motion, not once it is deployed. Coil Horizon Only qualifies while the gyratory sphere is in motion, not once it is deployed. Helminth-transferable Hildryn Balefire Pillage Only qualifies during the initial expansion Helminth-transferable Haven Aegis Storm Inaros Sandstorm Ivara Quiver Only qualifies while its arrows are in flight. Using Artemis Bow's alt-fire to loose Quiver's arrows does not qualify. Helminth-transferable Navigator Only qualifies while actively controlling a projectile. Prowl Briefly becoming visible while Prowl is active does not disqualify it. Artemis Bow Jade Glory on High Lavos Vial Rush Helminth-transferable Limbo Stasis Cataclysm Mag Magnetize Only the protective hold-cast version qualifies Mesa Ballistic Battery Peacemaker Mirage Prism Nekros Desecrate Nezha Divine Spears Nidus Larva Buff is lost on recasting, even if Larva grabs enemies. Helminth-transferable Parasitic Link Nyx Mind Control Helminth-transferable Absorb Oberon Hallowed Ground Only qualifies when Hallowed Eruption is used. Renewal Oraxia Silken Stride Qorvex Crucible Blast Only qualifies during the casting animation. Revenant Reave Helminth-transferable Danse Macabre Rhino Iron Skin Only qualifies when Iron Shrapnel is used. Sevagoth Gloom Helminth-transferable Temple Exalted Solo Titania Razorwing Valkyr Hysteria Wisp Wil-O-Wisp Only qualifies when tap-cast. Sol Gate Wukong Cloud Walker Defy Helminth-transferable Primal Fury Yareli Merulina Riptide Only qualifies when tap-cast. Helminth Parasitic Armor Tips Many Warframes lack channeled abilities altogether, making it impossible to use this Arcane with them. Certain abilities can be transferred via Helminth to circumvent this: Gloom can be infused onto any Warframe in order to give them a channeled ability and enable the use of this Arcane. Mind Control and Parasitic Armor are abilities with high base duration that can maintain this Arcane's effect without constant energy drain. Pillage, Coil Horizon and Quiver are also Helminth-compatible and can make use of this Arcane's secondary effect of instant healing upon ability activation. Patch History Hotfix 33.0.11 (2023-05-24) Fixed rare crash that would occur when viewing Arcane Intention while in the Arsenal. Update 33.0 (2023-04-26) Introduced.

sections.text: Arcane Momentum is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 60% chance to increase the Reload Speed for 12 seconds on Sniper-type weapons whenever the player achieves a Critical Hit. This effect's duration can be refreshed while active. Can be sold for 200 Credits 200. Effect Rank Reload Speed Bonus 0 +25% 1 +50% 2 +75% 3 +100% 4 +125% 5 +150% Acquisition Awarded for killing or capturing the Eidolon Teralyst. Dropped in Rotation C of Orphix Venus Proxima, Neptune Proxima, and Pluto Proxima missions. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Pluto Proxima Orphix / C 10% ~ 10 C Rotations 65 ± 21 C Rotations Neptune Proxima Orphix / C 10.34% ~ 9 C Rotations 63 ± 21 C Rotations Tier 1 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Venus Proxima Orphix / C 10.34% ~ 9 C Rotations 63 ± 21 C Rotations Tier 9 The Circuit (Normal) 4% ~ 25 A Rotations 169 ± 56 A Rotations Tier 3 The Circuit (Normal) 0.67% ~ 149 A Rotations 1027 ± 342 A Rotations Eidolon Teralyst Kill 14.95% ~ 6 Kills 42 ± 14 Kills Eidolon Teralyst Capture 12.05% ~ 8 Kills 53 ± 17 Kills All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. History Arcane Momentum was first introduced in the Trial mission The Jordas Verdict, which was retired in Update 22.14 (2018-03-01). The Operation: Scarlet Spear event sold Arcane Momentum from Little Duck for 1,000 Scarlet Credits. The Operation: Orphix Venom event sold Arcane Momentum from Father for 200 Phasic Cells. The Operation: Gargoyle's Cry event sold Arcane Momentum from the Vigile Jahu Gargoyle for 3 Grotesque Splinter. The Operation: Belly of the Beast event sold Arcane Momentum from Ordis for 1 Volatile Motes in the first run of the event, and 2 Motes in the second, with a purchase limit of 42. Notes Stacks additively with reload speed from mods such as Fast Hands. Can be triggered by critical hits from almost any source, including all weapon types, beast and sentinel attacks, and even Warframe abilities (such as Wisp's Breach Surge sparks and all of Gyre's damaging abilities) Tips Using a Sentinel weapon with a high sustained critical hit rate is a good way making use of this Arcane, such as: Prime Laser Rifle, Deth Machine Rifle, or Sweeper Prime. Sentinels such as Diriga and Djinn can be used for their increased attack range. Alternatively, applying zoom mods like Eagle Eye to a sentinel's weapon will also increase their attack range. It's recommended to avoid using mods like Vicious Spread or Heavy Caliber as sentinel weapons tend to already have low accuracy. It should be noted for stealth frames that going invisible will cause sentinels to stop attacking enemies. Patch History Hotfix 35.0.3 (2023-12-18) Fixed the amount of Vosfor received from dissolving Arcane Momentum being lower than intended. Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Momentum On Critical Hit: 60% (was 10%) chance for +25%/150% Reload Speed to Sniper Rifles for 12s (was 2s). Hotfix 27.3.12 (2020-04-14) Fixed Arcane Momentum not activating for Critical shots on the Condrix. Update 27.2 (2020-03-05) Added 2 maximum ranks Maximum chance to activate increased from 40% to 60% Maximum reload speed increased from +100% to +150% Update 22.12 (2018-02-09) Now affects Reload Speed with a base chance of 10% Hotfix 22.13.1 (2018-02-15) Fixed Arcane Momentum and Arcane Consequence reflecting incorrect text descriptions since their recent changes in U22.12.0. Update 17.9.1 (2015-10-29) Introduced with The Jordas Verdict.

sections.text: Arcane Pistoleer is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 60% chance on killing an enemy with a headshot from a secondary weapon to increase Ammo Efficiency by 102% for 12 seconds. Can be sold for ‍1,000 Credits ‍1,000. Effect Rank Ammo Efficiency Bonus 0 +17% 1 +34% 2 +51% 3 +68% 4 +85% 5 +102% Acquisition Awarded from Arbitrations missions. Can be purchased from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 10 Vitus Essence. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Arbitrations / A 5% ~ 20 A Rotations 134 ± 44 A Rotations Arbitrations / C 5% ~ 20 C Rotations 134 ± 44 C Rotations All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. Notes Directly reduces ammo cost by a percentage, while active. At rank 5 no ammo will be consumed. After being activated by the secondary, the buff also works on primary weapons. The buff doesn't affect special weapons, like the Thermian RPG. Getting another proc does not refresh the timer. The current instance of Arcane Pistoleer must run out before another can be started. In order for the arcane to activate enemy must be killed with direct damage, Gas or Electric status effects. Other damage over time effects will not trigger the buff. Alt-fire modes that shoot all remaining bullets in an automatic burst ( Cyanex, Nagantaka, or Pandero) shoot the current number of bullets left in the magazine while conserving them, allowing for an ammo-efficient, repeated use of these modes. Azima alt-fire still consumes the entire magazine as normal, but the turrets themselves benefit from partial ammo efficiency, expending their ammo more slowly and therefore lasting much longer. However, 100% ammo efficiency does not benefit turrets in any way and they will last their normal duration. Is also capable of being toggled by pistol Exalted Weapons like the Dex Pixia. Tips Can greatly improve the performance of weapons that otherwise have low magazine size like Akbronco ( Prime) or Twin Rogga. The same applies to primaries like ( Sancti) Tigris ( Prime), ( Kuva) Tonkor, and Vectis ( Prime). Synergizes well with Pyrana Prime's passive, allowing the wielder to make more use of the fire rate/akimbo buff without sacrificing the buff's duration to reloads. Patch History Hotfix 41.0.4 (2025-12-17) Fixed performance issues caused by the Akarius with Arcane Pistoleer equipped. Update 38.6 (2025-05-21) Arbitration rotation rewards (excluding Omni Forma) have been added to the Arbitration store (Arbiters of Hexis in the Relay) to provide players an easier path to these items via Vitus Essence. Update 31.6 (2022-06-09) ARCANE STAT CHANGES PER RANK We’ve adjusted the values of older Arcanes with the goal of making lower rank Arcanes more viable, and more desirable while collecting as a result. In short, there are three stats that each Arcane tends to have (speaking generally) -- for example, a percentage chance, a stat buff, and the duration of the buff. Certain Arcanes increase all of these stats per rank (making them scale exponentially), but we are changing it so that only one stat is affected by rank (making it scale linearly). The other stats will now be at the max-rank value regardless of the Arcane Rank, thereby buffing lower-rank Arcanes as a result. Arcane Pistoleer On Pistol Headshot Kill: 60% (was 10%) chance for +17%/102% Ammo Efficiency for 12s (was 2s) Update 30.9 (2021-11-11) Fixed weapons with 1 bullet in their magazine (such as Vectis and Exergis) bypassing the Fire Rate limit if equipped with a max rank Arcane Pistoleer. Hotfix 25.8.1 (2019-10-09) Fixed Arcane Pistoleer not giving infinite Ammo at 100% efficiency for weapons that fire more than one bullet at a time. Hotfix 25.7.7 (2019-09-26) Fixed Arcane Pistoleer not listing “+1 Arcane Revive” at max Rank. Hotfix 25.7.6 (2019-09-18) Introduced.

excerpt: Arcane Pistoleer is an Arcane Enhancement that provides a 60% chance on killing an enemy with a headshot from a secondary weapon to increase Ammo Efficiency by 102% for 12 seconds. Can be sold for ‍1,000 Credits ‍1,000. Effect Rank Ammo Efficiency Bonus 0 +17% 1 +34% 2 +51% 3 +68% 4 +85% 5 +102% Acquisition Awarded from Arbitrations missions. Can be purchased from the Arbitrations vendor in the Arbiters of Hexis room in Relays for 10 Vitus Essence. Item Source Chance Expected Nearly Guaranteed Arbitrations / A 5% ~ 20 A Rotations 134 ± 44 A Rotations Arbitrations / C 5% ~ 20 C Rotations 134 ± 44 C Rotations All drop rates data is obtained from DE's official drop tables stored on Module:DropTables/data. See Mission Rewards#Standard Missions for definitions on reward table rotations. For more detailed definitions and information, visit here. Notes Directly reduces ammo cost by a percentage, while active. At rank 5 no ammo will be consumed. After being activated by the secondary, the buff also works on primary weapons. The buff doesn't affect special weapons, like the Thermian RPG. Getting another proc does not refresh the timer. The current instance of Arcane Pistoleer must run out before another can be started. In order for the arcane to activate enemy must be killed with direct damage, Gas or Electric status effects. Other damage over time effects will not trigger the buff. Alt-fire modes that shoot all remaining bullets in an automatic burst ( Cyanex, Nagantaka, or Pandero) shoot the current number of bullets left in the magazine while conserving them, allowing for an ammo-efficient, repeated use of these modes. Azima alt-fire still consumes the entire magazine as normal, but the turrets themselves benefit from partial ammo efficiency, expending their ammo more slowly and therefore lasting much longer. However, 100% ammo efficiency does not benefit turrets in any way and they will last their normal duration. Is also capable of being toggled by pistol Exalted Weapons like the Dex Pixia. Tips Can greatly improve the performance of weapons that otherwise have low magazine size like Akbronco ( Prime) or Twin Rogga. The same applies to primaries like ( Sancti) Tigris ( Prime), ( Kuva) Tonkor, and Vectis ( Prime). Synergizes well with Pyrana Prime's passive, allowing the wielder to make more use of the fire rate/akimbo buff without sacrificing the buff's duration to reloads. Patch History Update 34.0 (2023-10-18) Base vs Final Stats in Modding - Health / Energy / Shield / Armor Stat Overhaul If you’ve spent any time invested in the deeper nuances of Modding, you may be familiar with “Warframe Math” - math that upon first glance doesn’t really make sense, but once you learn the inner workings of the game, it all comes together. While we can appreciate the value that complex systems offer to a certain subsect of players, there are other aspects of the game that should have clear and understandable outcomes. Namely: Shield, Health, Energy, and Armor Modding. Pop quiz: what is 300 + 440%? If you answered 740, you may just be an Excalibur player. Vitality (+440% Heath), Redirection (+440% Shields), Flow (+150% Energy), and Steel Fiber (+110% Armor) come with large modifier values that don’t seem to match their outcome in-game. This is because these Mods apply their multiplier to the base stats of the Warframe - i.e., the stats you have at Rank 0. In the Excalibur example, a Rank 30 Excalibur’s Health stat of 300 earns an additional 440 Health from max rank Vitality (+440% Heath) since it applies to his base rank Health stat of 100, resulting in 740 total health. In this update, we have removed this obfuscation by having Health, Shield, Energy, and Armor Mods apply to the stats of Warframes at their current rank. Continuing our Excalibur example, instead of Vitality always applying to Excalibur’s base rank 100 Health, it would apply to his Health stat based on his rank - namely, the stat you can actually see in your Arsenal. If your Excalibur were Rank 30, his Health stat would be 300, which means Vitality’s multiplier would be calculated off of 300. With previous Health and Mod values, additional adjustments are needed to make this revision work while maintaining game balance. By only changing where the multiplier applies, a Rank 30 Excalibur would receive an extra 1,320 Health from max rank Vitality, resulting in a total health stat of 1,600. This outcome is a significant buff, which is not the intention of this system change. To remedy this, we approached this problem in two ways: 1 - We reduced the overall multiplier for Health, Shield, Energy, and Armor Mods. Since these now affect Max Rank Warframe stats, these Mods need to scale differently to maintain the status quo. Additionally, we wanted these new values to be as clear and understandable to all players as possible! Here are a few examples of these value changes: Vitality: Reduced from +440% to +100% Health Redirection: Reduced from +440% to +100% Shield Capacity Steel Fiber: Reduced from +110% to +100% Armor Flow: Reduced from +150% to +100% Energy Max Doing some quick math, this means that a Rank 30 Excalibur (300 Health) with a reworked Vitality Mod (+100% Health, applied to the final Health stat) would receive 300 extra Health, for a total of 600. That, in contrast, is a nerf, which we also don’t want to do. So, our next step: 2 - We adjusted Warframe Health, Shield, Energy, and Armor values to keep the end result of the revised Mods as close to the original values as possible. With this change, Excalibur’s Rank 30 Health stat is 370. With +100% Health from a max Vitality Mod, his resulting Health stat would be 740, which matches what it was originally. While this path to the same result may seem a little complicated, the outcome matches our intention: we want players to be able to look at their Health, Shield, and Armor Mods, and be able to understand how they affect the stats they see in their Arsenal. In addition to everything above, we also increased the base stat values for Warframes so that these revised Mods offer similar value for lower-ranked Frames. To do so, we reduced the amount of Health/Shield/Energy that Warframes earn per rank in half, and transferred the sum of that value to their base stats. Mod Changes: Argon Plating: +100% Armor (was +90%) Update 15.0 (2014-10-24) Introduced.

sections.text: Fishing CODEX These fish hatch out of translucent pods formed in caverns below the Infested landscape. They hover to feed. Amniophysi is an infested fish in the Cambion Drift. Biome: Cave Activity: Fass/Vome Maximum Weight: 16 kg Rarity: Common Small Medium Large Benign Infested Tumor x3 Ferment Bladder x2 Waxen Sebum Deposit x3 Benign Infested Tumor x4 Ferment Bladder x3 Waxen Sebum Deposit x3 Benign Infested Tumor x8 Ferment Bladder x6 Waxen Sebum Deposit x3 Farming spots These are based on opinions and may not be 100% true. These should be viewed as advice for finding the resource until better facts are proven. Infested Seriglio Detailed walkthrough: Head in from the west entrance (the side closest to The Abscess). Go straight take a right and then an immediate left at the junction. There will be a small, secluded pool : throw in some bait (both work) and the Amniophysi will come out in a few moments. Amniophysi Trophy “A trophy of a prized catch.” —In-Game Description Amniophysi Trophy is a Fishing Trophy Decoration that can be crafted and displayed in the player's Orbiter. The blueprint can be purchased from Daughter for 1,500 Standing 1,500, requiring the rank of Stranger with the Entrati. Manufacturing Requirements 5,000 1 25 30 1,800 Time: 1 hrs Rush: 10 Platinum 10 Market Price: N/A Blueprints Price: 1,500 Gallery Concept art Patch History Update 29.0 (2020-08-25) Introduced. sections.text: “"A component of the Antiserum Injector"” —In-Game Description Antiserum Injector Fragment is a component that is used to make the Antiserum Injector for Infested Salvage mission. It is dropped by enemies during Infested Salvage mission. Found in quantities of 7. Unlike most other components, these can be sold for 5 Credits 5. Item Checklist for Antiserum Injector Fragment General Item Type Quantity Antiserum Injector Gear 500 Research Item Type Quantity Antiserum Injector Gear 600 Research x1 x3 x10 x30 x100 Last updated: Hotfix 38.0.12 (2025-02-20) Farming Locations This only drops from one location, the only Infested Salvage mission in the game. sections.text: Wild CODEX An avian of ridiculous appearance. Mergoo are odd, bird-like creatures native to the Plains of Eidolon on Earth. They loosely resemble prehistoric birds. They lack frontal wings, instead possessing an extra set of hind limbs giving them the appearance of a small feathery reptile equipped with an Archwing. They are carnivorous, hunting bugs and small fish, which they catch with incredible speed and precision. Subspecies Coastal Mergoo As the name suggests, Coastal Mergoo are commonly found around oceans and large bodies of water. It is considered extremely bad luck to kill one, as they can alert fishermen by squawking when marine predators, such as Lanx, are present. When captured via the Conservation will reward the following: Perfect Good Bad 1200 Standing 1200 900 Standing 900 600 Standing 600 Woodland Mergoo Adapted to life away from shores, these mergoo possess a green coat to blend in with their surroundings. Woodland Mergoo often know the locations of ant hills and grub colonies, making them just as important to hunters as their cousins are. When captured via Conservation the will reward the following: Perfect Good Bad 2400 Standing 2400 1800 Standing 1800 1200 Standing 1200 Splendid Mergoo Befitting their title, Splendid Mergoo sport a wonderful array of colors, causing Grineer troops to hunt them to near extinction. Curiously, they also interbreed regularly with other mergoo species, likely contributing to their rarity. When captured via Conservation the will reward the following: Perfect Good Bad 3600 Standing 3600 2700 Standing 2700 1800 Standing 1800 Trivia Mergoo have existed since at least 1999, as evidenced by an exhibit in the Princess Lucina Zoological Gardens. Mergoo bear a strong resemblance to the Avichaea found on the Cambion Drift. When entering the Orb Vallis during The New War, Mergoo will fly away from the elevator. Patch History Hotfix 31.7.1 (2022-07-28) Fixed motionless Mergoo models scattered on the ground in the Orb Vallis. Update 24.6 (2019-04-04) Introduced.

sections.text: CODEX A strong and highly aggressive subspecies that can engage groups of enemies with Viral jaws and incapacitating spores. A rare Predasite subspecies that only appears during Vome. Less skittish than the other subspecies, Medjay Predasites are more prone to charging if they feel threatened. Due to their Infested nature, Medjays possess an infectious bite, able to infect its targets with a highly-reactive virus. Additionally, they can shed clouds of paralytic spores when charging at foes. When captured via Conservation, they will reward the following: Perfect Good Bad 3x Medjay Predasite Tag 2x Medjay Predasite Tag 1x Medjay Predasite Tag
